steps.title: Identify the question type
steps.content: Transition question. Albert chose an unconventional surface (concrete) for graffiti; David has likewise chosen an unusual canvas (ice floes) for charcoal art. Both father and son made parallel unconventional artistic choices, so the transition must signal similarity.

steps.title: Evaluate each choice
steps.content:
• Choice A — However,: signals contrast. The two sentences describe similar artistic choices, not opposing ones. ✗
  • Choice B — Indeed,: signals emphatic confirmation of the previous statement. David's choice does not confirm or intensify Albert's; it presents a separate parallel case. ✗
  • Choice C — Second,: signals enumeration of items in a list. The first sentence is not framed as item one of a list. ✗
  • Choice D — Likewise,: signals similarity between two cases — exactly the parallel between Albert's concrete and David's ice floes. ✓
That matches Choice D — Likewise,.
